description: Build a production-ready Telegram bot that accepts expense information from text and images, extracts structured data, categorizes expenses accurately, stores records persistently, and provides correction/reporting workflows for personal finance tracking.

userStories.title: Bootstrap bot runtime and Telegram webhook/polling integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reliable Telegram bot runtime that receives updates and routes commands/messages so that all future expense features have a stable entry point.
acceptanceCriteria: Project has clear runtime entrypoint with environment-based configuration (token, mode, db/url, timezone, locale defaults).
Telegram bot starts successfully in development and production mode (polling and/or webhook supported per architecture choice).
Incoming updates are validated and routed by type (text, photo, command, callback query).
Health/logging baseline exists: startup logs, update-level structured logs, and fatal error handling.
Basic commands respond correctly: /start, /help, /ping (or equivalent diagnostics).
Typecheck/lint/test commands are wired and pass.
userStories.notes: Keep provider-specific code behind adapters to make testing simpler.

userStories.title: Create expense domain model and persistence layer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need normalized storage for users and expenses so captured data is durable and queryable for reports and corrections.
acceptanceCriteria: Database schema includes at minimum: users, expenses, and optional expense_attachments/source_metadata tables.
Expense entity supports: id, telegramUserId/chatId, amount, currency, merchant, category, spentAt, notes, sourceType(text|image), confidence, createdAt, updatedAt.
Migrations (or schema sync) run successfully from a clean environment.
Repository/service methods exist for create, update, delete, list by range, and recent expenses.
Timezone-safe date handling is implemented (store canonical UTC, format in user locale/timezone).
Typecheck passes and integration tests validate CRUD flows.
userStories.notes: Prefer deterministic IDs and explicit indexes for user/date queries.
